criteria.label: Payoff concreteness
criteria.notes: Names naive recursive Fibonacci, O(2^n) vs O(n), n=50 threshold, 'billions of operations'.

criteria.label: Staircase coherence
criteria.notes: Six rungs from diagnosis through both cures and decision rule. Minor: optimal-substructure rung 3 vs narration intro at 58s.

criteria.label: Destination precision
criteria.notes: Both conditions defined precisely; both techniques named; complexity implication stated.

criteria.label: Narration clarity
criteria.notes: Nearly every term defined on first appearance. Minor: 'edit distance' at t=225 introduced without unpacking.

criteria.label: Board specificity
criteria.notes: Every draw renderer-executable: tree, shortest-path diagram, memo DAG, complexity table. All write actions verbatim.

criteria.label: Board coherence
criteria.notes: Timeline well-ordered. Minor: complexity_naive and complexity_memo coexist briefly in top-right (160-170s).

criteria.label: Board visual balance
criteria.notes: Four draws plus highlights/writes. Excellent mix for graph/tree-heavy topic.

criteria.label: Math and notation correctness
criteria.notes: Recurrence, base cases, complexity all correct. Edit distance O(m*n) correct.

criteria.label: Code-content decision
criteria.notes: lru_cache snippet at t=143 idiomatic and correct. Granularity matches the beat.

criteria.label: Pacing and density
criteria.notes: Ten beats / 250s averages 25s/beat. Beat at 225s slightly rushed (two complexity analyses in 25s).

criteria.label: Redundancy and focus
criteria.notes: Stays focused. Mild redundancy: 40s beat partly echoes payoff framing.

topThreeIssues: Beat at 225s introduces 'edit distance' without definition for intermediate learners; either define briefly or replace with self-contained second example
Pacing at 225s is rushed: two distinct complexity analyses packed into one 25s beat
Optimal substructure appears third in staircase but is introduced first in narration; reorder staircase to match
summary: Strong plan earning near-full marks across all criteria: payoff quantified, staircase gap-free, board renderer-executable, code correct and well-timed. Only meaningful weaknesses are the undefined 'edit distance' term and slight rush in final complexity analysis.
